original: A meteorite of estimated 190kg mass explodes over Holbrook in Navajo County, Arizona, causing approximately 16,000 pieces of debris to rain down on the town
The Wesley Impact: black spot the size of Earth is discovered on Jupiter after unknown object crashes onto the planet
Largest intact sarcophagus of its kind ever found (2000 years old) opened in Alexandria, contains 3 skeletons, not a curse as feared
Scientists discover metals can self-heal after observing metals cracking and fusing back together, paving the way for future self-healing structures and vehicles
Percy Spencer, American physicist and inventor of the microwave, born in Howland, Maine (d. 1970)[1]
Tom and Jerry first appear under their own names in the cartoon "The Midnight Snack" byWilliam HannaandJoseph Barbera
Mansa Musa, ruler of the Mali Empire, arrives in Cairo on his way to Mecca, with a procession of 600,000 men, 12,000 slaves and 80 camels carrying 136 kg (300 pounds) of gold each
Christa McAuliffe chosen as first school teacher to fly aboard the space shuttle
Apollo 11 goes into lunar orbit
Airbus Beluga XL, painted to look like the whale, makes its first flight, landing in Toulouse-Blagnac, France
US scientists calculate the total amount of plastic ever produced: 8.3 billion tonnes, equal to the weight of 1 billion elephants
Thirteen sharpnose sharks are the first to test positive for cocaine off the coast of Rio de Janeiro, Brazil

Arthur Rankin, Jr, American director, producer, and screenwriter (Rankin/Bass Productions -The Year Without A Santa Claus; Rudolph The Red-Nosed Reindeer; Frosty The Snowman), born in New York City (d. 2014)
Max Fleischer, Polish-American animator and film producer (Betty Boop, Popeye the Sailor Man), born in Kraków, Austrian Poland (d. 1972)
USS Cod saves 51 sailors from Dutch sub in only sub-to-sub rescue
First Arab interplanetary mission, the Mars "Hope" orbiter launches on board Japanese H-2A rocket from Tanegashima Space Center
NASA's Apollo 18 and SovietSoyuz 19linked in orbit for 2 days, separate
Michael Brunet discovers the skull of Sahelanthropus tchadensis in the Djurab Desert, Chad, one of the oldest known species in the human family tree and 6-7 million years old
Archaeological dig in Kakadu National Park extends Aboriginal peoples time in Australia to 65,000 to 80,000 years ago
Steamship SS Great Britain is launched, designed byIsambard Kingdom Brunel, it is the first ocean-going craft with an iron hull or screw propeller and the largest vessel afloat in the world
AstronomerJohannes Keplerhas an epiphany and develops his theory of the geometrical basis of the universe while teaching in Graz, Austria
First US Army flying school for black cadets dedicated in Tuskegee, Alabama
American swimmerJohnny Weissmullerfirst to break 1 minute barrier for 100m freestyle; swims 58.6s at Alameda, CA
Babe Ruthhits his 30th home run of the 1920 baseball season, breaking his own single-season record. He would attain 54 throughout the season, before breaking that record the next year.
Rick Ferrell(Red Sox) andWes Ferrell(Cleveland Indians), two brothers on opposite teams, hit home runs in the same game, a first in MLB history
17-year-old Cleveland Indians future Baseball HOF pitcherBob Fellermakes his MLB debut in relief in 9-5 loss to Senators at Griffith Stadium, Washington
Shooting begins on Star Trek 2nd pilot "Where No Man Has Gone Before"
Teen comedy film "Clueless" is released, loosely based onJane Austen's book "Emma" starringAlicia Silverstone, directed by Amy Heckerling
"Captain America: The First Avenger", directed by Joe Johnston, starringChris Evansand Hayley Atwell, premieres in Los Angeles, California
The first three-dimensional reconstruction of a human head in a CT is published
Steven Van Slyke, American chemist, co-inventor of 1st practical organic light-emitting diode, born in Denver, Colorado[1]
Hollie Roffey, English youngest heart transplant at 10 days old, born in London  (d. 1984)
Bobby Murphy, Filippino-American software engineer and entrepreneur (co-founder of Snapchat), born in Berkeley, California
Widespread technology outage grounds flights and shuts down bank and media companies around the world, caused by an update error by cyber security firm CrowdStrike
Temperatures exceed 40°C in parts of England for first time
NASA civilian Test pilot Joe Walker in X-15 reaches 105 km
US launches Explorer 35 for lunar orbit (800/7400 km)
Soviet spacecraft Soyuz 14 lands back on earth
Richard E. Byrd, Laurence McKinley Gould, and their polar expedition team return to the United States following the first exploration of the interior of Antarctica
HMS Beagle withCharles Darwinarrives in Ascension Island
Russian ship Dmitri Donskoii, with possible cargo of gold coins is discovered in waters between South Korea and Japan where it sunk in 1905
Emma Abbott, a floating hospital for sick children, makes trial trip, NYC
NY Yankees sign their 1st Black players,Elston Howardand Frank Baines
New York Yankees catcher Thurman Munson's first-inning single and RBI are nullified because tar on his bat handle exceeds the 18-inch limit; Minnesota Twins win 2-1
Cal Ripkenplays in his 1,500th consecutive game
First game ever canceled at Seattle Kingdome due to falling tiles
First US women's rights convention is held in Seneca Falls, NY, organized byElizabeth Cady Stantonand Lucretia Mott
First commercial flight, the "bird of peace" between Ethiopia and Eritrea in 20 years lands in Asmara, Eritrea, reuniting families
Largest wind farm in Africa opens at Lake Turkana, Kenya, generating 310 megawatts
Missouri woman Sandra Hemme released from prison after her 43 year conviction for murder was overturned - longest known wrongful conviction of a woman in US history
Circus Maximus in Rome catches fire
BBC World Service begins playing V(ictory) ("...-" in Morse code) (opening ofLudwig van Beethoven's 5th symphony)
British PMWinston Churchilllaunches his "V for Victory" campaign
Elvis Presley's debut single, a cover of Arthur Cruddup's "That's All Right" is released
World Formula 1 drivers championLewis Hamiltonwins a record 8th Hungarian Grand Prix in Budapest to equal Michael Schumacher's record of wins at a single circuit (French GP)
Geraldine Ferraro(US Representative, New York), becomes the 1st US female major-party vice-presidential nominee, officially joiningWalter Mondale's Democratic Party ticket
Tim Witherspoon KOsFrank Brunoin 11 for heavyweight boxing title
"Despacito" by Luis Fonsi featuringDaddy Yankeebecomes the most streamed song ever, played 4.6 billion times
1st female to captain a 747 across Atlantic (Lynn Rippelmeyer)
1st in-flight movie shown on TWA
